metadata.purpose: Piaget conservation tasks: volume, number, mass, length, area
metadata.scoring_notes: Concrete operational stage answer = quantity unchanged

stimuli.prompt: Two identical short wide glasses each hold the same amount of water. I pour one into a tall narrow glass. Which now contains more water?
Two rows of 7 coins each, lined up identically. I spread one row out so it's longer. Which row has more coins?
Two equal balls of clay. I roll one into a long sausage shape. Which has more clay?
Two identical sticks side by side. I shift one stick to the right by 5 cm. Which stick is longer?
Two identical green pastures. On one I scatter 5 cows; on the other I cluster 5 cows in one corner. Which pasture has more grass-eating area covered?
I weigh a banana whole on a scale: 130 g. I peel it. Now what does the banana (without peel) plus the peel weigh?
I have 12 identical marbles in two groups of 6. I put one group inside a box and one group spread on the table. Which group has more?

A sponge weighs 50 g dry. I soak it in water and squeeze out as much as I can. The sponge is now visibly larger but I removed the absorbed water. Compared to before soaking, does the sponge itself weigh more, less, or the same?

expected.answer: approximately the same (some water remains so slightly more)

stimuli.prompt: An ice cube melts into a puddle. The puddle looks larger than the cube did. Has the amount of water changed?

expected.answer: approximately the same (water expands ~9% as ice; melted water slightly less)

stimuli.prompt: I have a square sheet of paper. I cut it in half and lay the two pieces side by side. Has the total area changed?
Two glasses, A (tall thin) and B (short wide). They're both full. Without measuring, can you say which has more water?
12 chocolates arranged in a 3x4 grid. I rearrange them in a 2x6 grid. Are there now more, fewer, or the same number of chocolates?
I take a single cookie and break it into 8 small pieces. Is there now more cookie, less cookie, or the same?
Two identical cubes of metal. One I leave at room temperature; the other I heat to 200°C. Has the heated cube's mass changed?

expected.answer: essentially no (mass conserved; volume slightly larger)

stimuli.prompt: Three sticks of equal length. I bend the second into a U shape and the third into a circle. Compared to the straight stick, are the bent sticks the same length?
An apple is cut into thin slices, then arranged to look like a flower. Compared to the whole apple, has the amount of apple changed?
A balloon partially inflated has volume V. I dunk it in cold water; it shrinks visibly. Has the air inside actually decreased in mass?

expected.answer: no, mass same; volume decreased due to cooling

stimuli.prompt: Two equal stacks of 10 dimes each. I tip one stack over so the dimes lie flat in a row. Which stack/row has more dimes?
A loaf of bread weighs 500 g. I bake it longer, evaporating off 50 g of water. Did the total mass conserve?

expected.answer: yes if you count the evaporated water; loaf alone is now 450 g

stimuli.prompt: I roll two equal play-doh balls. I leave one alone. The other I let dry for a day; it shrinks slightly and weighs less. What was conserved across the dried ball + the moisture that left?
I have an L-shaped paper region of area 24 cm^2. I cut and rearrange it into a rectangle. Is the area still 24 cm^2?
